title: Corrective fix for B-422d9d91: Validate planner sizing and followup behavior end-to-end
description:
Blocked reason: `test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children` fails: after completing a developer bead inside a feature tree with explicit shared tester/documentation/review beads already depending on that developer, the scheduler still creates legacy child followups (`<bead>-test`, `<bead>-docs`, `<bead>-review`).

Remaining work: Fix scheduler followup creation so a completed developer bead under a planner/feature root reuses existing shared tester/documentation/review beads that already depend on that developer, instead of creating legacy child beads like `<developer>-test`, `<developer>-docs`, and `<developer>-review`. Re-run the targeted orchestrator tests after the scheduler change.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B-422d9d91.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` so `_uses_planner_owned_followups` now treats direct children of a feature root inside an epic/planner tree as eligible for shared followups, even when that feature root is stored as a normal developer bead. Verified the file compiles with `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` and that `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` succeeds.
handoff_summary.remaining: Tester agent should rerun the targeted orchestrator regression covering shared followup reuse, then retry the parent corrective flow if validation passes.
handoff_summary.risks: No tests were run per developer-agent guardrails. The change broadens planner-owned followup reuse for developer beads whose parent is the feature root inside an epic-created tree; targeted scheduler regression coverage should confirm there are no unintended matches in adjacent feature-tree layouts.
handoff_summary.next_action: Have the tester agent rerun the targeted shared-followup scheduler regression and then retry the parent bead if the regression passes.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low conflict risk if downstream agents avoid editing scheduler followup selection logic concurrently.

conflict_risks: Low conflict risk if downstream agents avoid editing scheduler followup selection logic concurrently.

execution_history.summary: Adjusted scheduler followup reuse detection so developer beads directly under an epic-created feature root reuse planner-owned shared tester/documentation/review beads instead of falling back to legacy child followups.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
